content: A biological or chemical agent can be modeled as a small, goal-directed Neural Turing Machine adapted to dynamic, molecular-scale environments, where external cellular or solvent conditions act as the NTM’s external memory tape to store intermediate reaction and interaction states. This framing allows us to predict how simple biological agents can evolve to execute algorithm-like behaviors (such as sequential binding, sorting molecular substrates, or associative recall of target binding patterns) purely from input interaction examples.
